LAW AND MOTION 302, SUZIE S. THORN'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO DISMISS THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF DEFENDANT-CROSS-COMPLAINANT DANIEL L. MITCHELL, FOLLOWING ORDER TO SUSTAIN DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. THE COURT WILL ENTER AN ORDER TO DISMISS THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE COURT BASES ITS ORDER ON THE GROUNDS THAT ON MAY 1, 2015, THE COURT ENTERED JUDGMENT IN FAVOR OF SCHAPIRO-THORN INC. ON THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, THAT ON SEPTEMBER 10, 2015, THE COURT SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, MS. THORN'S DEMURRER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, AND THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT NAMES NO CROSS-DEFENDANTS OTHER THAN SCHAPIRO-THORN INC. AND MS. THORN. PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 581(F)(1), THE COURT MAY DISMISS A COMPLAINT AFTER DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AND EITHER PARTY MOVES FOR DISMISSAL. MR. MITCHELL'S NOTICE OF APPEAL DOES NOT DIVEST THE COURT OF JURISDICTION TO ISSUE SUCH AN ORDER BECAUSE AN ORDER SUSTAINING A DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IS NOT AN APPEALABLE ORDER. (WOOD V. ELLING CORP. (1977) 20 CAL.3D 353, 357, FN. 1; SISEMORE V. MASTER FINANCIAL, INC. (2007) 151 CAL.APP.4TH 1386, 1396; MORTGAGE GUARANTEE CO. V. LEE (1943) 61 CAL.APP.2D 367, 373.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Defendant Suzie S. Thorn, For An Order To Dismiss The Cross-Complaint Of Defendant-Cross-Complainant Daniel L. Mitchell, Following Order To Sustain Demurrer Without Leave To Amend
Off Calendar Nov-12-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM M. HENLEY OF WILD CAREY & FIFE, TEL. NO. 1-415-837-3101 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS SCHAPIRO-THORN INC. and SUZIE S. THORN'S Motion For An Order To Dismiss The Cross-Complaint Of Defendant-Cross-Complainant Daniel L. Mitchell, Following Order To Sustain Demurrer Without Leave To Amend is granted. The court will enter an order to dismiss the cross-complaint in its entirety. The court bases its order on the grounds that on May 1, 2015, the court entered judgment in favor of Schapiro-Thorn Inc. on the cross-complaint, that on September 10, 2015, the court sustained, without leave to amend, Ms. Thorn's demurrer to the cross-complaint, and that the cross-complaint names no cross-defendants other than Schapiro-Thorn Inc. and Ms. Thorn. Pursuant to California Civil Procedure section 581(f)(1), the court may dismiss a complaint after demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend and either party moves for dismissal. Mr. Mitchell's notice of appeal does not divest the court of jurisdiction to issue such an order because an order sustaining a demurrer without leave to amend is not an appealable order. (Wood v. Elling Corp. (1977) 20 Cal.3d 353, 357, fn. 1; Sisemore v. Master Financial, Inc. (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 1386, 1396; Mortgage Guarantee Co. v. Lee (1943) 61 Cal.App.2d 367, 373.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. WILLIAM M. HENLEY, ESQ. of Wild, Carey & Fife, Tel No. 415-837-3101 appearing for Cross-Defendant Suzie S. Thorn;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT SUZIE S. THORN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend. When a plaintiff makes allegations against an employee who was not a party to an agreement which includes an arbitration provision and the employee is inextricably intertwined with these obligations, the employee is entitled to the benefits of the arbitration provision. (Goldman v. KPMG LLP (2009) 173 Cal. App.4th 209; Dryer v. Los Angeles Rams (1985) 40 Cal. 3d 406, 418.) Here, the Cross-Complaint states: "[a]t all times herein mentioned," Ms. Thorn "was the agent and employee" of Shapiro-Thorn, Inc. "[A]t all times herein mentioned," Ms. Thorn "was' acting within the scope of said agency and employment." (Cross-Complaint 2.) All of the allegations included in the Cross-Complaint arose from the scope of Ms. Thorn's employment by Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Shapiro-Thorn, Inc. Therefore, because all claims in the Cross-Complaint were denied with respect to Shapiro-Thorn, Inc., Ms. Thorn, as an employee of Shapiro-Thorn, Inc., is entitled to the benefit of the arbitration award providing that all of Mr. Mitchell's claims against Shapiro-Thorn are denied. Therefore the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

CROSS-DEFENDANT SUZIE THORN'S Motion To Set Aside Default Of Cross-Defendant Suzie Thorn With Respect To The Cross-Complaint Of Defendant Cross-Complainant Daniel L. Mitchell is granted. The default entered against Cross-Defendant Suzie Thorn is set aside. "Section 128.5'was superseded for actions filed on or after January 1, 1995 by section 128.7." (Clark v. Optical Coating Laboratory, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 150, 164.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
